rulings.Case Title: JOANNA L. PFEISTER VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 12, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER JOANNA PFEISTER Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs is granted in part. Petitioner is awarded attorney fees of $15,498, paralegal fees of $225, and costs of $1,280.14. The attorney fees are calculated at the reasonable hourly rate of $410 multiplied by the reasonable number of hours of 37.8. The reasonable number of hours is determined to be: 8 hours for the work performed from February 1 - March 6, 2023; 8 hours for the work performed from March 23-30, 2023; 4.8h ours for the work performed April 3 and 4, 2023; 5 hours for the work performed April 6-18; and 12 hours for the work performed May 23, 2023, to the present. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. RICHARD KIWATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Confirmation Of Referees Report Re Apportionment Of Fees And Expenses And Distribution Of Proceeds Of The Sale Of Real Property Discharge Of Referee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 12, 2023 line 3. OTHER GERARD KEENA II Petition For Confirmation Of Referees Report Re Apportionment Of Fees And Expenses And Distribution Of Proceeds Of The Sale Of Real Property Discharge Of Referee is OFF CALENDAR. The requested supplemental report has not been filed and it does not appear that the purported June 28, 2023 hearing has been set/calendared in this action. Referee may re-file this request addressing all the issues previously raised by the Court and when the issue of additional attorney's fees has been conclusively resolved. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 12, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT JENNIFER SPALDING Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Continue Trial is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and CCP 1013. rulings.Case Title: ANTON HALTEH VS. DAVID MORGANSTERN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 12, 2023 line 9. DEFENDANT DAVID MORGANSTERN DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly allege which part of the TPA 2019 this tenancy is subject to. The Court notes that both section 7 and 8 of the Form Complaint refer to only CC 1946.2 and plaintiff check the box 8(a) regarding just cause. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Notice includes complete information regarding payment under CCP 1161(2) as to at least one person to whom the payment should be made.=(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
